html:
Sundart is no longer the screaming 2-3x earnings idea implied by older drafts, but it is still an unusually cheap, ignored Hong Kong small-cap with a balance sheet the market is not capitalizing properly. At a last quoted price around HKD 0.72, it trades at about 5.8x FY2025 EPS, 0.43x book, and roughly a 7% final dividend yield, while holding more cash and pledged deposits than its market cap before working-capital liabilities.

The real variant view is narrower and cleaner than “Macau-dominant contractor.” FY2025 revenue was still heavily split across PRC, Hong Kong, Macau, Singapore, and materials, with PRC revenue of HKD 2.30 billion barely profitable, Hong Kong producing most of the economic profit, Macau doubling from a small base, and a new Philippines materials order showing high-margin optionality. That mix matters. Sundart is not yet a pure Macau recovery story. It is a misclassified contractor where the market sees China property receivable risk, but the current earnings engine is shifting toward Hong Kong execution, Macau refurbishment/casino capex, and higher-margin decorative materials.

The latest filing is good enough to make the equity interesting. FY2025 revenue fell 14.4% to HKD 5.14 billion, but the company still earned HKD 267 million, EPS of HKD 0.1238, and proposed a HKD 0.05 final dividend equal to a 40.4% payout. It ended 2025 with HKD 1.99 billion of bank balances and cash, HKD 138 million of pledged deposits, only HKD 41 million of bank borrowings, and book equity of HKD 3.62 billion. Outstanding works were HKD 6.78 billion at year-end, giving 2026 revenue visibility if execution holds. The official annual report is here: Sundart FY2025 annual report.

The market’s weak read is understandable but probably too punitive. FY2025 included HKD 57.8 million of expected-credit-loss charges, over-90-day trade receivables remain meaningful, and PRC work remains a poor-margin drag. There was also a May 2026 placing of 22 million shares at HKD 0.66, but that is only about 1% dilution and appears tied to restoring public float rather than funding distress. The company was below the 25% minimum public float, and the placing mathematically fixes that problem while raising only about HKD 14 million of net proceeds. That announcement is here: May 2026 placing.

The upside torque comes from modest normalization, not heroic assumptions. If Sundart converts its HKD 6.8 billion backlog into HKD 5.5-6.0 billion of 2026 revenue, holds gross margin around 13-14%, and reduces credit losses by even HKD 25-40 million, net income can move back toward HKD 330-380 million. That is EPS of roughly HKD 0.15-0.17 after the small placing, putting the stock at about 4.2-4.8x forward earnings. At a normal 7-8x earnings multiple for a cash-rich but controlled contractor, the stock is worth HKD 1.20-1.40 before giving much credit for excess cash.

The Macau angle is real but should be sized correctly. Macau’s concessionaires are committed to a decade-long non-gaming investment program, originally MOP 108.7 billion, and 2026 demand is still recovering: Macau visitor arrivals rose 13.7% year over year to 11.2 million in Q1 2026, while April 2026 gross gaming revenue rose 5.5% year over year and the first four months were up about 12%. Sundart’s Macau revenue was only HKD 520 million in 2025, so this is option value, not the base business. The upside case is that Macau refurbishment and non-gaming capex become a larger mix contributor while Hong Kong stays profitable and materials distribution scales.

The stock is also ignored. There is little analyst support, trading is thin, and retail discussion is almost nonexistent compared with larger Hong Kong yield and recovery names. That matters because a HKD 1.5-1.6 billion market cap contractor with a near-7% dividend, net cash, and a visible order book can rerate sharply if two clean reporting periods show stable receivables and no new PRC credit accident.

The bear case is capital allocation and receivables. Sundart has the cash to pay dividends, but it also has a history of large working-capital exposure, related-party complexity, concentrated control, and a willingness to issue stock below market even with abundant liquidity. If cash is trapped, PRC receivables worsen, or Macau/HK awards disappoint, the market will keep treating book value as low quality.

My base case is that Sundart earns HKD 350 million in 2026, pays a 40% payout dividend, and rerates to 8x earnings as public float is repaired and the market stops pricing it as a distressed China property supplier. My bull case needs Macau and materials to matter more, plus Singapore or Hong Kong margin recovery, taking earnings power toward HKD 500-550 million. My bear case assumes another credit-loss cycle and a market discount to cash because governance trust remains weak.

Bear target: HKD 0.45, down 38% from HKD 0.72.

Base target: HKD 1.45, up 101%.

Bull target: HKD 2.50, up 247%.
